# Buffett Essays Symposium Transcript

The document summarizes a 1996 symposium where Warren Buffett and Charlie Munger discussed issues of corporate governance with experts. It notes their views on topics like director duties, CEO evaluation, and acquisitions remain relevant. The author recently republished an annotated transcript of the event. It highlights Buffett's emphasis on boards prioritizing good CEO selection and replacement. Despite enormous growth at Berkshire Hathaway, Buffett and Munger have maintained consistent views over 20 years. Their ability to distill complex issues and stay grounded underscores their wisdom for directors.
